title: After three heatwaves, French people worry: 'Next year will be worse. How will we cope?'
subhead: Across France, people of all ages have realized how vulnerable they are to extreme temperatures, which have stunned the country during these past weeks.

Wildfire haze hangs over eastern US ahead of World Cup final
subhead: The wildfires burning in Canada have sent noxious smog to settle over much of the eastern United States. At one point on Saturday, the smoke made New York the  most polluted city in the world – only one day before the World Cup final is to be held across the river in New Jersey in an open-air stadium.

title: 'We are trapped in a climate dystopia, a black comedy scenario, where the grotesque rivals the Orwellian farce'
subhead: As heatwaves multiply, public debate often amounts to little more than empty gestures. Recent government decisions to cut green funds and return energy-inefficient homes to the rental market reveal the denial prevailing among much of the political class, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Foucart.

title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London for forthcoming display at British Museum
subhead: The thousand-year-old, 70-meter-long embroidery depicting William the Conqueror's exploits in medieval England has been removed from its crate after its transfer from France. Staff will begin a thorough check of its condition on Monday, and it will be placed in a custom-made display case a few days later.

title: How Dakar caught surfing's new wave: 'It has become a path to success and a way of life'
subhead: The Senegalese coast, with its small villages and beaches where Atlantic swells create powerful waves that draw surfers from around the world, has produced a new generation of young champions eager to ride. Every day, they take on the ocean at about 12 spots along this wild, little-known shoreline.

title: Behind the scenes of Macron's last G7, as told by photographer Kamil Zihnioglu
subhead: There is protocol, and then there is Donald Trump. That, in essence, is what photographer Kamil Zihnioglu took away from his three days covering the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains for Le Monde.

title: The troubling Russian connections of a 'European' password manager
subhead: Although it presents itself as a Spanish company, Passwork was actually developed in Russia and operates with a license from Russia's FSB domestic intelligence agency. The situation raises major security questions for its customers, including several French companies.
